name: @plexui/ui
description: The most flexible UI kit for React. Production-grade components with pixel-perfect Figma parity.

components.name: Alert
components.description: Call attention to a specific message or warning

props.name: indicator
props.description: Custom indicator or false to hide

examples.name: Overview
examples.code:
<Alert
  title="Scheduled maintenance"
  description="We'll be offline 2 - 4 AM UTC on July 14 while we upgrade our database."
  actions={<Button color="primary" variant="soft" pill>Dismiss</Button>}
/>

examples.name: Colors & variants
examples.code:
<Alert
  color="primary"
  variant="outline"
  title="Scheduled maintenance on Saturday, 2-4 AM PT"
  description={<>Track status updates on <TextLink color="currentcolor" href="#">status page</TextLink></>}
/>
examples.details: You can stylize the display of the alert using the color and variant props.

examples.name: Title
examples.code: <Alert title="Your current access level limits what you can view or modify" />
examples.details: You can optionally include a title for your alert with the title prop.

examples.name: Description
examples.code:
<Alert
  description={
    <>
      We're working on centralizing SCIM and invite settings. For now, setup is handled within individual product settings. <TextLink href="#">Learn more</TextLink>
    </>
  }
/>
examples.details: You can optionally include description text for your alert with the description prop.

examples.name: Actions
examples.code:
<Alert
  variant="soft"
  color="warning"
  title="Password expires in 3 days"
  description="Update it now to avoid losing access to your account."
  actions={<Button color="primary" pill>Update password</Button>}
/>
examples.details: You can add actions to the alert with the actions prop, which accepts ReactNode. Combine with onDismiss to add a dismiss button.

examples.name: Action placement
examples.code:
<Alert
  title="Our terms of service has been updated"
  description="We've updated our terms to clarify how we handle data, billing, and user permissions. Please review and accept the latest terms to avoid impacting your service."
  actions={(
    <>
      <Button color="primary" variant="soft" pill>Set reminder</Button>
      <Button color="primary" variant="solid" pill>Review terms</Button>
    </>
  )}
  actionsPlacement="bottom"
/>
examples.details: Actions are automatically positioned in the alert by default. You can force a specific position with the actionsPlacement prop by passing either 'end' or 'bottom'.

examples.name: Indicator
examples.code:
<Alert
  indicator={<Lightbulb />}
  description={
    <>
      We're working on centralizing SCIM and invite settings. For now, setup is handled within individual product settings. <TextLink href="#">Learn more</TextLink>
    </>
  }
/>
examples.details: Alert provides an indicator icon by default. You can override it by passing a custom icon to indicator, or pass indicator={'{false}'} to hide it entirely.

examples.name: Dismissible
examples.code:
<Alert
  className="items-start"
  title="Try our new dashboard layout"
  description="We've introduced a streamlined layout that makes using the dashboard even easier. You can switch back any time."
  actions={(
    <>
      <Button color="primary" variant="soft" pill>Dismiss</Button>
      <Button color="primary" variant="solid" pill>Try it</Button>
    </>
  )}
/>
examples.details: The suggested approach for dismissible notices is to use labeled buttons via actions. You can also provide onDismiss to render a dismiss button in the alert chrome.

components.name: Avatar Group
components.description: Display avatars as a single stack

props.name: size
props.description: Size of all avatars in the group

examples.name: Overview
examples.code:
<AvatarGroup size={42}>
  <Avatar name="Tyler" imageUrl="https://gravatar.com/avatar/xyz" />
  <Avatar name="Jane" color="primary" variant="solid" />
  <Avatar name="Tech support" Icon={Robot} variant="solid" />
  <Avatar overflowCount={5} />
</AvatarGroup>

examples.name: Stacking direction
examples.code: <AvatarGroup stack="end" size={42}>...</AvatarGroup>
examples.details: You can control the stacking direction using the stack prop, as either "start" or "end".

examples.name: Group sizing
examples.code:
<AvatarGroup size={48}>
  <Avatar name="Tyler" color="info" />
  <Avatar name="Jane" color="discovery" />
  <Avatar name="Will" color="danger" />
  <Avatar overflowCount={5} />
</AvatarGroup>
examples.details: You can control the size of all avatars in the group by passing size directly to the <AvatarGroup> component.

components.name: Avatar
components.description: Display user identities with either text, photo, or an icon

props.name: name
props.description: Display name; used for text initial when no image/icon

examples.name: Overview
examples.details: A variety of avatar types: text, image, icon, and overflow.

examples.name: Text
examples.code: <Avatar name="David" size={48} />
examples.details: For text-based avatars, a single initial is displayed based on the name prop passed to the component.

examples.name: Image
examples.code:
<Avatar
  name="Tyler"
  imageUrl="https://gravatar.com/avatar/9531b260b9693f3394bea8646c6ea141ce58fe5a138b7db7729d60a4c5dde552"
  size={48}
/>
examples.details: To display an image avatar, provide an absolute URL to the image with imageUrl. Images will have a soft fade entrance once loaded, or fallback to a text display if the image fail to load.

examples.name: Icon
examples.code:
<Avatar
  Icon={Robot}
  size={48}
/>
examples.details: To display an icon avatar, provide an icon to render to the Icon prop. The name prop is not used in this case, and can be omitted.

examples.name: Overflow count
examples.code: <Avatar overflowCount={9} size={48} />
examples.details: To display an overflow avatar, provide a number to the overflowCount prop. The number will be automatically formatted and scaled to fit.

examples.name: Sizing
examples.code: <Avatar name="David" size={48} />
examples.details: To size the avatar, pass a number to the size property, which will set the width and height in pixels. Text and icon avatars will scale their sizes dynamically based on this value.

examples.name: Colors & variants
examples.details: You can stylize the display of the avatar using the color and variant props.

examples.name: Roundness
examples.code: <Avatar name="Acme, co." color="primary" variant="solid" size={48} className="rounded-lg" />
examples.details: By default, avatars are fully rounded. You can override this by passing a Tailwind rounding class to className.

examples.name: Interactive
examples.code:
<Avatar
  name="Will"
  size={48}
  onClick={saySup}
/>
examples.details: Avatars can be used as interactive elements by passing an onClick handler. The avatar will automatically apply semantic and visual interaction styles.

components.name: Button Link
components.description: "Button as a semantic anchor element"

props.name: disabledTone
props.description: Use relaxed visual tone for disabled state

props.name: className
props.description: Custom classes for the root element

examples.name: Overview
examples.code: <ButtonLink color="primary" pill={false} href="#">View API Keys <ArrowRight /></ButtonLink>

examples.name: Internal link
examples.code:
<ButtonLink href="/components/button" color="primary">
  Internal link <ArrowRight />
</ButtonLink>
examples.details: When a relative href or to is provided, ButtonLink automatically uses the configured LinkComponent from PlexUIProvider so your router handles navigation and prefetching.

examples.name: External link
examples.code:
<ButtonLink href="#" color="primary">
  External link <ArrowUpRight />
</ButtonLink>
examples.details: When linking to an external URL (or when external is set), <a> is used with target="_blank" and rel="noopener noreferrer" added automatically. If your link is not being automatically identified, you can force external link behavior with the external prop.

examples.name: Colors & variants
examples.code: {/* Matrix of variant x color */}
examples.details: Stylize the link with color and variant props.

examples.name: Sizing & roundness
examples.code:
<ButtonLink size="lg" pill={false}>
  View account <ArrowRight />
</ButtonLink>
<ButtonLink size="lg" pill={false}>
  View account
</ButtonLink>
examples.details: Sizing of the link is controlled with size and gutterSize props. size sets the control height, and gutterSize sets the horizontal padding. Create fully rounded links with the pill prop. When pill is set, extra horizontal gutter is dynamically applied to the link.

examples.name: Icons
examples.code:
<ButtonLink color="info" size="lg" iconSize="lg" variant="soft" href="#">
  <Globe /> View website
</ButtonLink>
examples.details: Icon sizing is controlled by the iconSize prop, which makes it easy to add icons as children without additional props. size will provide a default value for iconSize, but setting the prop will force the specified size.

examples.name: Block
examples.code: <ButtonLink block size="lg" href="#">Continue to dashboard</ButtonLink>
examples.details: Create full-width links with the block prop.

examples.name: Disabled
examples.code:
<ButtonLink href="#" color="primary" disabled>
  View API Keys
</ButtonLink>
examples.details: Use disabled to both visually and accessibly disable a link. The component renders as a span[role="link"], and onClick handlers are not bound.

components.name: Button
components.description: Create actions in many different styles

props.name: size
props.description: Control height and default gutter/icon size

examples.name: Overview
examples.details: A showcase of Button's key styles: solid, soft, outline, ghost, and icon-only.

examples.name: Colors & variants
examples.code: ( )} />
examples.details: You can stylize the display of the button using the color and variant props.

examples.name: Sizing & roundness
examples.details: Control height with size and horizontal padding with gutterSize. Use pill for fully rounded corners. size sets the button to a specific control height, and gutterSize sets the horizontal padding. Each size provides default values for gutterSize and iconSize props, as well as font-size. Each of these values can be overridden independently. When pill is set, extra horizontal gutter is dynamically applied to the button.

examples.name: Icons
examples.code:
<Button color="secondary" size="lg" uniform variant="ghost">
  <PlusLg />
</Button>
examples.details: Add icons as children — sizing is automatic via iconSize prop. Default values for iconSize are provided through the size prop, but you can pass the prop directly to change the defaults. For icon-only buttons, there are two common approaches: Square — create perfect square icon buttons with the uniform prop. When uniform is passed, gutterSize is ignored. Combine with pill to make circular icons. Tighter gutters — applying a smaller gutterSize than size can create well-sized, but not exactly square, icon buttons.

examples.name: Block
examples.details: Create full-width buttons with the block prop.

examples.name: Disabled
examples.code: <Button disabled>Submit</Button>
examples.details: Use disabled to both visually and accessibly disable a button.

examples.name: Inert
examples.code: <Button inert>Submit</Button>
examples.details: Use inert to accessibly disable a button without any visual changes. Note: For accessibility reasons, disabled is used instead of applying inert directly to the button. Visual disablement in the disabled prop is applied via [data-disable].

examples.name: Loading
examples.code: <Button loading size="xl" pill><ArrowUp /> Click to load</Button>
examples.details: Use loading to display a loading indicator in the button, fading out the current content. Interactions will be prevented by default during this state, by providing a default value for inert to true. You can override this behavior directly with inert, or use disabled to add visual disablement.

examples.name: Selected
examples.code:
<Popover>
  <Popover.Trigger>
    <Button selected variant="solid">Click to open</Button>
  </Popover.Trigger>
  <Popover.Content minWidth="auto" className="p-4 text-sm">
    <ShimmerText className="font-medium text-secondary">Button should look selected</ShimmerText>
  </Popover.Content>
</Popover>
examples.details: Use selected to indicate that a given button is correctly in an open or selected state. The exact visual behavior differs across each variant. This behavior is automatically configured with components like Menu that apply [aria-expanded="true"] or [data-state="open"] attributes.

examples.name: Optical alignment
examples.code:
Default gutters
opticallyAlign="start"
examples.details: Use opticallyAlign with either "start" or "end" to offset the gutter of the button.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label — accepts ReactNode for rich content

props.name: className
props.description: CSS classes applied to wrapper node

props.name: id

examples.name: Overview
Variants
examples.details: The checkbox supports three visual styles: solid (default) with a filled background and standard radius, solid pill with fully rounded corners, and ghost which renders only the checkmark without a border or background fill.

examples.name: Pill
examples.code: <Checkbox label="Checked" pill defaultChecked />
examples.details: Set pill to render the checkbox indicator with fully rounded corners (border-radius: full) instead of the default square radius.

examples.name: Ghost
examples.code: <Checkbox label="Checked" variant="ghost" defaultChecked />
examples.details: Use variant="ghost" to render the checkbox without a border or background. The checkmark uses the text color instead of white, making it suitable for subtle inline selections or task lists.

examples.name: With description
examples.code:
Accept terms and conditions By checking this box, you agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

examples.details: The label prop accepts ReactNode, so you can compose a label with secondary description text. This is useful for options that need additional context or explanation.

examples.name: Indeterminate
examples.details: Use checked="indeterminate" to indicate a partially selected state. This is typically used for "select all" controls where only some child items are checked.

examples.name: Disabled
examples.details: When disabled is set, the checkbox is dimmed to 50% opacity and prevents interaction. Works consistently across all variants — solid, pill, and ghost.

examples.name: Label orientation
examples.code: <Checkbox label="Accept terms and conditions" orientation="right" />
examples.details: By default the checkbox renders to the left of the label. Set orientation="right" to place the checkbox after the label.

examples.name: Without label
examples.details: When no label is provided, the checkbox renders as a standalone control. All three visual states are shown: unchecked, checked, and indeterminate.

examples.name: Group with select all
examples.details: Combine a parent checkbox with child checkboxes to create a "select all" pattern. The parent shows an indeterminate state when only some children are checked.

components.name: Date Input
components.description: Masked text input for typing dates with automatic formatting and validation

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called when a complete valid date is entered or the field is cleared

props.name: mode
props.description: Date format mode: dd/mm/yyyy, mm/dd/yyyy, yyyy/mm/dd, etc.

props.name: timeMode
props.description: Enable time input: HH:MM, HH:MM:SS, etc.

props.name: dateTimeSeparator
props.description: Separator between date and time parts

props.name: clearable
props.description: Show clear button when input has a value

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Auto-generated from format if not set

examples.name: Overview
examples.code:
const [date, setDate] = useState<DateTime | null>(null)
<DateInput value={date} onChange={setDate} clearable />

examples.name: With time
examples.code: <DateInput value={date} onChange={setDate} timeMode="HH:MM" clearable />
examples.details: Pass timeMode="HH:MM" to enable date + time input in one field.

examples.name: With seconds
examples.code: <DateInput timeMode="HH:MM:SS" ... />
examples.details: Use timeMode="HH:MM:SS" for second-level precision.

examples.name: Limits
examples.code:
<DateInput
  min={today.minus({ days: 30 }).startOf('day')}
  max={today.plus({ days: 30 }).endOf('day')}
  clearable
/>
examples.details: Use min and max to restrict the date range. The mask prevents entering values outside the allowed range.

examples.name: US format
examples.code: <DateInput mode="mm/dd/yyyy" separator="/" ... />
examples.details: Set mode="mm/dd/yyyy" and separator="/" for US-style date formatting.

examples.name: ISO format
examples.code: <DateInput mode="yyyy/mm/dd" separator="-" ... />
examples.details: Set mode="yyyy/mm/dd" and separator="-" for ISO 8601 formatting.

examples.name: With Field
examples.code:
<Field label="Date of birth" description="Enter your date of birth">
  <DateInput value={date} onChange={setDate} clearable />
</Field>
examples.details: Wrap with Field to add a label, description, and automatic accessibility attributes.

examples.name: Validation
examples.code:
<Field label="Date of birth" errorMessage={!date ? 'Date is required' : undefined}>
  <DateInput value={date} onChange={setDate} clearable />
</Field>
examples.details: Pass errorMessage to Field to display a validation error.

examples.name: Disabled
examples.code: <DateInput disabled value={DateTime.local(2025, 1, 1)} onChange={...} />

components.name: Date Picker
components.description: Select a single date with an interactive calendar

props.name: captionLayout
props.description: Calendar header mode. Use dropdown for date of birth

props.name: triggerDateFormat
props.description: Date format in trigger. Auto-includes time when showTime is enabled

examples.name: Overview
examples.code:
const [selectedDate, setSelectedDate] = useState<DateTime | null>(null)
<DatePicker value={selectedDate} onChange={setSelectedDate} clearable pill />

examples.name: Limits
examples.details: Use min and max to limit selectable dates to a specific timeframe.

examples.name: Date of birth
examples.code:
<DatePicker
  captionLayout="dropdown"
  triggerDateFormat="MMMM d, yyyy"
  max={DateTime.local()}
  placeholder="Select your birthday"
  clearable pill
/>
examples.details: Set captionLayout="dropdown" to add month and year dropdowns inline with the arrow navigation. Ideal for selecting a date of birth where navigating month-by-month would be impractical.

examples.name: Time picker
examples.code:
<DatePicker
  showTime
  triggerDateFormat="MM/dd/yy HH:mm"
  placeholder="Select date & time"
  clearable pill
/>
examples.details: Enable showTime to add hour and minute inputs below the calendar. The popover stays open after selecting a date so the user can adjust the time. Use arrow keys to increment or decrement values.

examples.name: Dropdown + time
examples.code:
<DatePicker
  captionLayout="dropdown"
  showTime
  yearRange={[2020, 2030]}
  triggerDateFormat="MMM d, yyyy HH:mm"
  placeholder="Schedule event"
  clearable pill
/>
examples.details: Combine captionLayout="dropdown" with showTime for quick month/year jumps and time selection in a single picker — useful for scheduling events at a specific date and time.

examples.name: Two fields
examples.details: Compose DatePicker and Input type="time" side by side for maximum flexibility in form layouts. Each field operates independently while sharing the same DateTime value.

examples.name: Date input
examples.code: <MaskedDateInput value={date} onChange={setDate} />
examples.details: A masked input for typing a date directly. Auto-formats as MM / DD / YYYY with segment highlighting and separator auto-advance.

examples.name: DateTime input
examples.code: <MaskedDateTimeInput value={dateTime} onChange={setDateTime} />
examples.details: Extended masked input for date and time. Format: MM / DD / YYYY HH : MM. Same auto-formatting behavior with time segments appended.

examples.name: Without Icon
examples.code: <DatePicker triggerShowIcon={false} placeholder="Choose a date" ... />
examples.details: Remove the calendar icon from the trigger by setting triggerShowIcon to false.

examples.name: Disabled
examples.code: <DatePicker disabled value={...} onChange={...} />
examples.details: Disable the picker to prevent user interaction.

components.name: Field
components.description: Form field wrapper with label, description, error, and accessibility linking

props.name: description
props.description: Helper text below the label; linked via aria-describedby

props.name: errorMessage
props.description: Error message below the control; auto-sets aria-invalid on child

props.name: size

examples.name: Overview
examples.code:
<Field label="Email">
  <Input placeholder="Enter email..." />
</Field>
examples.details: Field wraps a form control with a label, optional description, and error message. It automatically generates htmlFor, aria-describedby, and aria-invalid linking between the label, description, error, and child control.

examples.name: With description
examples.code:
<Field label="Email" description="We'll never share your email with anyone else.">
  <Input placeholder="Enter email..." />
</Field>
examples.details: Use description to display helper text below the label. The description is automatically linked to the control via aria-describedby.

examples.name: With error message
examples.code:
<Field label="Email" errorMessage="Please enter a valid email address">
  <Input placeholder="Enter email..." />
</Field>
examples.details: Use errorMessage to display a validation error. When provided, the child control automatically receives aria-invalid="true". The error is rendered using the FieldError component with role="alert" for accessibility.

examples.name: Required
examples.code:
<Field label="Email" required>
  <Input placeholder="Enter email..." />
</Field>
examples.details: Use required to show a visual asterisk after the label. This is purely visual and does not add the required HTML attribute — pass required directly to the child control for form validation.

props.description: Controls gutter on the edges; defaults to value from size

props.description: Disables the input visually and from interactions

props.description: Allow autofill extensions to appear in the input

props.name: autoSelect
props.description: Select all contents of the input when mounted

props.name: startAdornment
props.description: Content rendered at the start of the input

props.name: endAdornment
props.description: Content rendered at the end of the input

props.name: onClear
props.description: When provided, displays a clear button that appears when the input has a value

props.name: opticallyAlign
props.description: Applies a negative margin using the current gutter to optically align with surrounding content

examples.name: Overview
examples.code: <Input placeholder="Enter text..." />

examples.name: Variants
examples.details: Use variant to stylize the input.

examples.name: Sizing & roundness
examples.code: } size="lg" /> setUsername(evt.target.value)} maxLength={16} endAdornment={{username.length}/16} />
examples.details: Sizing of the input is controlled with size and gutterSize props. size sets the input to a specific control height, and gutterSize sets the horizontal padding. Each size provides default values for gutterSize, as well as font-size. Each of these values can be overridden independently.

examples.name: Start adornment
examples.code:
<Input
  placeholder="Start adornment"
  startAdornment={<Search className="fill-tertiary" />}
/>
examples.details: You can add arbitrary content to the beginning of an input with startAdornment – like a search icon. Ideally, the spacing will look pretty good by default. If you need to customize the spacing: gutterSize determines the amount of spacing on the edges of the container, which you can fine-tune as needed (if you need precise spacing not supported by gutterSize, use negative left margin on your adornment directly, e.g. -ml-2). The spacing between the adornment and the input is controlled with gap on the container, which you can customize as needed with Tailwind classes.

examples.name: Clear button
examples.code:
<Input
  placeholder="Enter text..."
  value={value}
  onChange={(evt) => setValue(evt.target.value)}
  onClear={() => setValue("")}
  pill
/>
examples.details: Use the onClear prop to display a built-in clear button. The button appears when the input has a value and is not disabled or read-only.

examples.name: End adornment
examples.code: setUsername(evt.target.value)} maxLength={16} endAdornment={ {username.length}/16 } />
examples.details: You can add arbitrary content to the end of an input with endAdornment – like a character counter or custom icon. The same spacing guidance from startAdornment applies here.

examples.details: Combine endAdornment with a toggle button to switch between type="password" and type="text". Works across all sizes, variants, and with pill.

  </Menu.Trigger>

examples.details: Menu supports several sizing strategies:Natural sizing – use minWidth="auto" to have the menu fit naturally to the size of the content.Fixed size – use width and minWidth to set the menu to a fixed size, regardless of content size.Flexible sizing – minWidth can be used by itself to describe a menu that must be a given size, but can grow larger depending on its content.Max sizing – apply max-width to the children of Menu.Content. maxWidth isn't exposed as a prop because it's reserved for Radix variables used in collision detection.

examples.name: Open on hover

<Popover showOnHover>
  <Popover.Trigger>...</Popover.Trigger>
  <Popover.Content>...</Popover.Content>
</Popover>
examples.details: Use showOnHover to create popovers that appear on hover and focus events. This should be used sparingly and with clear intention.By default, hover popovers will not alter the focused element on the page, nor restore focus to the trigger on close. However, if interactive content is present in the popover, and tab is used to navigate into the popover, focus will be restored to the trigger on close.

examples.name: Using the controller
examples.code:
const { close, shake } = usePopoverController()
// e.g. close after form submit, shake on validation error
examples.details: You can access usePopoverController() controller context from components within Popover.Content. The hook exposes two methods, close() and shake(). They do exactly what you think they do.Attempting to submit an empty form calls the shake() methodAfter async submission occurs (faked loading state), the close() method is called to close the popup imperatively.

components.name: Tabs
components.description: Toggle through grouped options with segmented or underline styles

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style — segmented (background highlight) or underline (line indicator)

props.name: orientation
props.description: Layout direction of the tab group

props.name: flush
props.description: Text-width indicator, no border, gap spacing (underline variant only)

props.name: badge
props.type: number | { content: ReactNode; color?: string; variant?: string }

examples.name: Overview
examples.code:
const [tab, setTab] = useState("overview")
<Tabs value={tab} onChange={setTab} variant="underline" aria-label="Sections">
  <Tabs.Tab value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="analytics">Analytics</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="reports">Reports</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Tabs provides two visual variants: segmented (default) with a sliding background highlight, and underline with an animated line indicator. Both support horizontal and vertical orientations.

examples.name: Segmented with icons
examples.code:
<Tabs size="lg" value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Navigation">
  <Tabs.Tab value="home" icon={<Home />}>Home</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="settings" icon={<SettingsCog />}>Settings</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="notifications" icon={<Bell />}>Notifications</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Icons work with the segmented variant — use the icon prop on individual tabs. Icons are automatically sized to match the current size.

examples.name: Icon only
examples.code:
<Tabs value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Navigation">
  <Tabs.Tab value="home" icon={<Home />} aria-label="Home" />
  <Tabs.Tab value="settings" icon={<SettingsCog />} aria-label="Settings" />
  <Tabs.Tab value="notifications" icon={<Bell />} aria-label="Notifications" />
</Tabs>
examples.details: Omit the text children and pass only the icon prop for compact icon-only tabs. Always provide an aria-label on each tab for accessibility.

examples.name: Segmented with badge
examples.code:
<Tabs size="lg" value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Filter">
  <Tabs.Tab value="all" badge={128}>All</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="unread" badge={{ content: 12, color: "info" }}>Unread</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="flagged" badge={{ content: 3, color: "danger" }}>Flagged</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Pass a number or an object with content, color, and variant to the badge prop. Badges render inline after the tab label inside the segmented container.

examples.name: Underline
examples.code:
<Tabs variant="underline" value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Sections">
  <Tabs.Tab value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="analytics">Analytics</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="reports">Reports</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="settings">Settings</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Set variant="underline" for a minimal tab style with an animated line indicator under the active tab. No background container is rendered.

examples.name: Underline flush
examples.code:

  <Tabs.Tab value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="analytics">Analytics</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="reports">Reports</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="settings">Settings</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Add flush with variant="underline" for minimal tabs where the indicator matches the text width exactly. No container border is rendered, and spacing between tabs uses gap instead of padding.

examples.name: Underline block
examples.code:
<Tabs variant="underline" block value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Sections">
  <Tabs.Tab value="overview">Overview</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="analytics">Analytics</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="reports">Reports</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Use block with the underline variant for full-width tabs that stretch evenly across the container.

examples.name: Underline with icons
examples.code:
<Tabs variant="underline" size="lg" value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Navigation">
  <Tabs.Tab value="home" icon={<Home />}>Home</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="settings" icon={<SettingsCog />}>Settings</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="notifications" icon={<Bell />}>Notifications</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Icons work with the underline variant the same way as with segmented. Use the icon prop on individual tabs.

examples.name: Underline with badge
examples.code:
<Tabs variant="underline" size="lg" value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Filter">
  <Tabs.Tab value="all" badge={128}>All</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="unread" badge={{ content: 12, color: "info" }}>Unread</Tabs.Tab>
  <Tabs.Tab value="flagged" badge={{ content: 3, color: "danger" }}>Flagged</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>
examples.details: Badges are supported with the underline variant. Pass a simple value or an object with content, color, and variant.

examples.name: Vertical segmented

examples.name: Vertical underline with icons

  <Tabs.Tab value="notifications" icon={<Bell />}>Notifications</Tabs.Tab>
</Tabs>

examples.name: Disabled tab
examples.code:
<Tabs variant="underline" value={tab} onChange={setTab} aria-label="Sections">

examples.code: const [value, setValue] = useState("Line 1\\nLine 2\\nLine 3")

examples.details: Sizing of the textarea is controlled with size and gutterSize props. size sets the textarea to a specific control height for single-line equivalent, and gutterSize sets the horizontal padding.Each size provides default values for gutterSize, as well as font-size and border-radius. Each of these values can be overridden independently.

You decide how this state is removed, whether that's through onFocus, onChange, an onSubmit handler, etc.Default visual styles will be applied, based on the variant. Additionally, [data-invalid] is applied to the container for custom styling.

examples.name: Auto select text
examples.details: Pass autoSelect to select all text in the textarea – useful when pairing with other copy actions. This prop behaves like an imperative action when toggled and allows selection to change.

examples.name: Autofill extensions

examples.details: Compact tooltips are designed for short, 1-3 word tooltips with inverted styling. They feature minimal padding, a dark background with white text, and no box shadows. Use {'compact={true}'} for brief status messages.
